sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a small, persistent rash on his face, which he attributes to the combination of the face wash and metro cream. He states the doxycycline has helped, but the rash hasn't completely resolved. He reports using the face wash twice daily and the metro cream immediately after washing his face. He denies using any other lotions or moisturizers. He also reports feeling a slight tingling sensation when applying the metro cream, but it quickly subsides.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Examination reveals a mild perioral dermatitis with a few erythematous papules and mild redness around the nostrils. The patient's skin type is IV, indicating a tendency to tan rather than burn. Blood pressure is slightly low. Heart rate is 78 bpm and regular. Neurological exam is unremarkable.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The primary problem is perioral dermatitis with possible rosacea features. The patient's symptoms suggest a possible inflammatory response to the combination of the face wash and metro cream.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe doxycycline 100mg twice daily for one week, then once daily for an additional week. Recommend applying metrocream to the affected area twice daily after washing the face. Advise the patient to use vanicream cleanser once daily and to moisturize with petroleum jelly if needed. Schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess response and adjust treatment as necessary. Advise patient to avoid using any other skincare products until further evaluation.
